paper_title: Remotely Sensed Data Interpretation and Topographic Survey; Sediment Sampling and OSL Dating

methods.method_name: Remotely Sensed Data Interpretation and Topographic Survey
methods.description: This method involves interpreting aerial photographs and satellite images to locate swales or lowlands likely to preserve ancient storm deposits, followed by field checks and topographic surveys to confirm data interpretation.

steps.description: Aerial photographs and satellite images of Kui Buri area were interpreted to locate swales or lowlands most likely to preserve ancient storm deposits.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret aerial photographs and satellite images to locate swales

steps.description: To confirm the interpretation of the data from aerial photographs and satellite images, field checks were performed after finishing the coastal geomorphologic interpretation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform field checks to validate remote sensing data

steps.description: Beach profiles were surveyed using a total station survey camera SOKKIA (SET630R), where surveying was performed at sea level and measured westwards across the outer beach ridge, swale and inner beach ridge respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: survey beach profiles using total station camera

steps.description: Elevation were converted to meters a.m.s.l. by following the method of Williams et al. (2016) using tide tables obtained from the nearest tide gauge stations.
steps.searchable_summary: convert elevation to meters above mean sea level

methods.description: This method involves sediment sampling and optically stimulated luminescence (OSL) dating to analyze ancient storm layers and establish the age of beach ridge formations.

steps.description: Site reconnaissance was conducted by a gouge core to estimate the depth of the potential ancient storm layer under the subsurface at around 2 m depth.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct site reconnaissance using gouge core

steps.description: Coring was applied to retrieve the stratigraphy along three transects in the swale perpendicular to the shoreline.
All sample cores retrieved from the swale were sealed by duct tape and then transported to the sedimentology lab for analyzing and sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: transport and prepare core samples for analysis

steps.description: Samples were analyzed using the laser granulometric method, and the data were computed using the logarithmic method of moments.
Sub-samples were dried in an oven at 70°C for 24 h, and then dry-sieved through a 63 µm sieve to gain a sand fraction without finer particles.
steps.searchable_summary: dry and sieve sub-samples to obtain sand fraction

steps.description: OSL samples were collected for establishing the lateral age of the beach ridge formation.
steps.searchable_summary: collect OSL samples for age dating

steps.description: The OSL analysis of the ED samples was performed using a Risø OSL/TL reader equipped with a calibrated 90Sr/90Y beta radiation source.
steps.searchable_summary: perform OSL analysis using Risø reader
